channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Reseña de ‘The Boroughs’: como ‘Stranger Things’, pero con adultos mayores
item.description: En una nueva serie de Netflix de la productora de los hermanos Duffer, los que luchan contra los monstruos ya no se mueven tan rápido como antes.
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale

item.title: Trump-Backed Ken Paxton Ousts Incumbent Senator in Texas
item.description: Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general endorsed by President Trump, defeated John Cornyn, a four-term Republican senator, in a runoff. The race’s results reflected Trump’s influence over the party.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times

item.title: Los científicos descartaron un escenario climático alarmante. ¿Y ahora qué?
item.description: Un equipo internacional de investigadores publicó una revisión de los escenarios de emisiones empleados para estudiar el calentamiento global. Esto provocó un intenso debate público.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer and Eric Niiler

media:content.media:credit: Johny Magallanes/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Un niño intentando refrescarse durante una ola de calor en Tegucigalpa, Honduras, este mes.

item.title: ¿Qué hacer si tienes una picadura de medusa? Orinar sobre ella quizá no sea la respuesta
item.description: Abundan los mitos y conceptos erróneos sobre las medusas. Aquí te explicamos cómo mantenerte a salvo esta temporada de playa cerca de estas maravillas gelatinosas.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

media:content.media:credit: Anadolu
media:content.media:description: Una medusa en el Acuario Steinhart de la Academia de Ciencias de California, en San Francisco. Las medusas desempeñan importantes funciones en el ecosistema marino y son una fuente clave de alimento para algunos peces y tortugas marinas.

item.title: El Centro Kennedy se queda en silencio
item.description: Las cancelaciones por motivos políticos y un cierre previsto para renovar el edificio han golpeado a la orquesta del Centro Kennedy, cuyos miembros no han recibido un salario completo en meses.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Towfighi
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Jared Soares para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Las decenas de músicos de la Orquesta de la Ópera del Centro Kennedy enfrentan un futuro incierto. “Fue como si nos hubieran dado un puñetazo en el pecho”, dijo David Jones.

item.title: Las guerras en Ucrania e Irán son más parecidas de lo que crees
item.description: Las incursiones de Rusia en Ucrania, y las operaciones de Israel y EE. UU. en Irán proporcionan lecciones sobre la evolución de la guerra moderna.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Roman Pilipey/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Evaluación de los edificios dañados tras los ataques rusos en la capital de Ucrania, Kiev, el domingo.

item.title: The Battle Over Trump’s Immigration Crackdown Comes to a Newark ICE Facility Parking Lot
item.description: For five days, activists have gathered outside a detention center in New Jersey to denounce conditions inside. Federal officials have rejected those concerns.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley and Mark Bonamo

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Demonstrators confronted federal agents on Tuesday outside the Delaney Hall immigration detention center in Newark.

item.title: For Years, Democrats Refused to Primary Him. This Year Is Different.
item.description: Thomas DiNapoli, the state comptroller in New York since 2007, now faces his first primary challenge, as two Democrats hope to capitalize on anti-incumbent fervor.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The New York State comptroller, Thomas DiNapoli, left, is facing his first primary challenge since taking office in 2007. His challengers are Drew Warshaw, center, and Raj Goyle, right.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 27, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘shibboleth’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 4 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: El silencio ante la violación de palestinos
item.description: Hombres y mujeres palestinos describen abusos sexuales brutales a manos de guardias de prisiones, soldados, colonos e interrogadores israelíes.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kristof
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Samar Hazboun para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Suhaib Abualkebash

item.title: Word of the Day: shibboleth
item.description: This word has appeared in 4 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Slams Trump for Missing His Son’s Wedding
item.description: “It was a small ceremony with about 40 guests, none of whom were his father,” the late-night host said of Donald Trump Jr.’s wedding in the Bahamas over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix

item.title: Every New Cease-Fire in Lebanon Brings Hope — Until It Doesn’t
item.description: In Lebanon, each new cease-fire is met with blind optimism — as if it hails the end of a conflict instead of what it actually is: an admission ticket to the next war.
item.dc:creator: Rana Hanna

media:content.media:credit: Peter van Agtmael/Magnum Photos

item.title: La arena temporal de la UFC comienza a tomar forma en el jardín de la Casa Blanca
item.description: El evento de la Ultimate Fighting Championship está previsto para el 14 de junio, día del cumpleaños 80 del presidente Trump.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Construcción en el jardín sur de la Casa Blanca el lunes, en víspera de los actos del 250 aniversario de Estados Unidos que incluyen el próximo combate de la UFC.

item.title: Cubans Deported to Mexico Live a Precarious Existence, Report Finds
item.description: A Human Rights Watch report found that many Cubans deported to Mexico by the Trump administration are living in an “indefinite legal limbo” and struggling to get by.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: More than 4,300 Cubans were deported from the United States to Mexico between President Trump’s 2025 inauguration and March 9, 2026.

item.title: Nigel Farage’s Far-Right Party Is Now Central to British Politics
item.description: Nigel Farage’s anti-immigrant, populist agenda has helped his party, Reform U.K., emerge from the fringe of politics in Britain. But it faces an uphill climb to win power.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:credit: Paul Reid/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Nigel Farage, the leader of the Reform U.K. Party, last month at a campaign rally in Scotland ahead of British council elections.

item.title: Where Time Is Always 15 Minutes Apart From Everywhere Else
item.description: In Nepal, a nation wedged between India and China, a unique time zone is just one expression of a singular national identity.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Hannah Reyes Morales

media:content.media:description: Ghanta Ghar, or the “Hour House,” during its renovation in Kathmandu. Nepal keeps its own time zone, 5 hours and 45 minutes ahead of Coordinated Universal Time.

item.title: Inventor of the Basque Cheesecake Plans to Retire. His Secret: He Prefers Chocolate
item.description: Santiago Rivera is widely credited with creating the “burnt” cheesecake in the 1980s, though he doesn’t love the spinoffs it has spawned. Decades later, he’s preparing to hand over his kitchen to his children.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz

media:content.media:credit: Markel Redondo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Santiago Rivera working the counter at his restaurant, La Viña, in San Sebastián, Spain this month.

item.title: Xi Jinping Quit Smoking. China Still Cannot.
item.description: China’s tobacco monopoly has become so financially vital to the government that even its powerful leader has failed to curb the country’s smoking habit.
item.dc:creator: Joy Dong

media:content.media:credit: Gilles Sabrie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: China has made slow progress curbing tobacco use or enacting a national indoor smoking ban.

item.title: Corrections: May 27, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, May 27, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: A 21st-Century First Has the Knicks’ Fans Caught Up in Euphoria
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, May 27, 2026.

item.title: Colin Allred, Former Lawmaker, Wins Democratic Runoff for House Seat
item.description: Mr. Allred beat the incumbent, Representative Julie Johnson, and is now favored to win the general election in a heavily Democratic Dallas-based district.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Colin Allred served three terms in the House of Representatives before giving up his seat to run unsuccessfully for the Senate in 2024.

item.title: 4 Takeaways From the ‘Summer House’ Season 10 Reunion
item.description: The highly anticipated episode delved into the timeline of the relationship between Amanda Batula and West Wilson and the pair’s confrontation with Ciara Miller and the rest of their housemates.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Clifton Prescod/Bravo
media:content.media:description: Kyle Cooke and Ciara Miller on the “Summer House” Season 10 reunion.

item.title: Cornyn Crushed: 7 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Runoffs in Texas
item.description: President Trump’s record of ousting those he sees as disloyal continued apace with Senator John Cornyn’s defeat. Whether his relationship with Senate Republicans can be repaired is another question.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton, the Republican attorney general of Texas, defeated Senator John Cornyn in a runoff Tuesday.

item.title: Trump-Backed Candidate Wins G.O.P. Race in San Antonio District
item.description: Carlos De La Cruz, a first-time candidate, defeated a moderate rival in a U.S. House district that was redrawn to favor Republicans, but where Democrats see an opportunity.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Michael Gonzalez/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Carlos De La Cruz, a Republican running in Texas’ 35th Congressional District, speaking in February before an appearance by President Trump in Corpus Christi, Texas.

item.title: Park Slope Food Co-op Votes to Boycott Israeli Products
item.description: In a three-hour meeting, members of the grocery cooperative voted by a margin of more than 2 to 1 in favor of a boycott.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The vote over whether to boycott Israeli products has torn apart the Park Slope Food Co-op in Brooklyn.

item.title: Johnny Garcia Beats Texas Democrat Accused of Being Antisemitic in House Runoff
item.description: The victory by Johnny Garcia, a sheriff’s deputy, brought relief to Democrats, who hope to hold onto a seat that Republicans redrew to flip it.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Callaghan O'Hare for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Johnny Garcia, a sheriff’s deputy, was the preferred candidate of Democratic leaders in Washington.

item.title: Mayes Middleton Defeats Chip Roy in the Runoff for Texas Attorney General
item.description: After connecting his opponent to past criticism of President Trump, the conservative state senator now advances to the general election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Shafkat Anowar/The Dallas Morning News, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Mayes Middleton in March at the Conservative Political Action Conference in Grapevine, Texas.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 27, 2026
item.description: Dario Salvucci meets us halfway.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: What Ken Paxton’s Win Means for James Talarico
item.description: The Texas senate race will come down to the Democratic candidate’s strengths — and the Republican’s weaknesses.
item.dc:creator: Jack Herrera

media:content.media:credit: Brenda Bazán/Associated Press

item.title: Minneapolis Police Chief Brian O’Hara Resigns After Investigation
item.description: The chief, Brian O’Hara, began leading the department in 2022 as it reeled in the aftermath of the police killing of George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño and Mitch Smith

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Brian O’Hara at a ceremony for new recruits in January.

item.title: Ken Paxton Ousts John Cornyn, Solidifying Trump’s Grip on Republican Party Voters
item.description: Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, overcame scandals and a significant fund-raising disadvantage to win. His victory sets up the general-election clash that Democrats had hoped for.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy, J. David Goodman and Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton, the attorney general of Texas, at his watch party in March in Plano. Mr. Paxton went on to defeat Senator John Cornyn in the Republican primary runoff election.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Ken Paxton, the Republican Senate Nominee in Texas
item.description: With the support of President Trump, Mr. Paxton unseated Senator John Cornyn in a high-profile Republican runoff.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, received an endorsement last week from President Trump.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About John Cornyn, Republican Senate Hopeful in Texas
item.description: He is fighting for political survival against a primary challenger backed by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator John Cornyn, Republican of Texas, narrowly led in the first round of primary voting in March.

item.title: Christian Menefee Defeats Al Green in Texas Democratic House Runoff
item.description: The showdown, manufactured by a Republican gerrymander, created one of many generational clashes consuming Democrats this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Christian Menefee became the first Democrat to defeat an incumbent this year, felling 11-term Representative Al Green.

item.title: Evacuation Orders Lifted as Southern California Chemical Tank Cools
item.description: The risk of an explosion or spill has fallen significantly, the authorities said late Tuesday, and all evacuated residents can now go home. Some have begun to ask who should be held accountable.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler and Rebecca Fairley Raney

media:content.media:credit: Jae C. Hong/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Barricades block a street in the evacuation zone in Garden Grove, Calif.

item.title: As Republicans Battled in the Runoff, Talarico Sought to Unify Democrats
item.description: James Talarico, the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate, has been raising money and looking to unite his party for the general election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: State Representative James Talarico of Texas at a rally in Austin after the primary election in March.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Al Green, Democratic House Candidate in Texas
item.description: He is a veteran lawmaker and a vocal critic of President Trump who is facing a runoff against a fellow Democratic member espousing generational change.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Al Green, Democrat of Texas, during a committee hearing in February.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Christian Menefee, Democratic House Nominee in Texas
item.description: Mr. Menefee, one of the newest members of Congress, beat Representative Al Green, a Democrat who is more than twice his age, in a primary runoff.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Annie Mulligan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Christian D. Menefee during a campaign event in March.

item.title: Donald E. Newhouse, Low-Profile Heir to a Media Empire, Dies at 96
item.description: He ran the newspaper division of Advance Publications, while his older brother, Si, ran the Condé Nast magazine operation.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

media:content.media:credit: Danny Moloshok/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Donald E. Newhouse in 2020. At the peak in 1999, Advance-owned newspapers had a combined circulation of 2.9 million, putting the company in third place behind Gannett and Knight Ridder.

item.title: Trump May Appear at N.B.A. Finals in New York
item.description: President Trump is said to be considering the appearance after his hometown team, the Knicks, clinched a championship spot.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:credit: David Richard/Imagn Images, via Reuters Connect
media:content.media:description: Members of the New York Knicks celebrating as Eastern Conference champions on Monday in Cleveland.

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. Keeps Picking Up Snakes. Is He Doing It Right?
item.description: The health secretary wrangled two nonvenomous snakes while visiting Florida in a video that drew widespread attention.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Portland Press Herald/Portland Press Herald, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A black racer snake. It is not venomous and has a relatively weak bite, experts said.

item.title: When to Expect Results in the Texas Primary Runoff
item.description: Unofficial results will start coming in after polls close at 7 p.m. local time. They will be posted on the state’s voter portal.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Texas voters are casting ballots in a variety of primary runoffs on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Administration Wants Employees to Sign NDAs
item.description: Lawyers representing federal workers said the move is intended to chill speech and could be challenged on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Sullivan

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Office of Personnel Management drafted a proposal that federal employees sign a nondisclosure agreement.

item.title: Manny Fernandez, Bulwark of Dolphins’ Defense, Dies at 79
item.description: He played a critical part in Miami’s 17-0 season in 1972, which ended with his spectacular defense in Super Bowl VII against Washington.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

media:content.media:credit: Focus on Sport/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Manny Fernandez, No. 75 of the Miami Dolphins, on the sidelines during a game in Miami in the early 1970s.

item.title: High-Level British Spy Warns of Expanding Russia Threat
item.description: The director of Britain’s electronic surveillance agency warns that Russia is only getting more brazen as battlefield losses in Ukraine mount.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman

media:content.media:credit: Igor Ivanko/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: President Vladimir Putin appearing on a screen during the Victory Day celebrations in Moscow earlier this month.

item.title: ‘Chess’ Will Close With Lea Michele’s Departure, in a Change of Plans
item.description: The Broadway musical had planned to keep going, but box-office sales made clear Michele’s appeal was essential. It will now close June 21.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lea Michele in the revival of “Chess” now running at Broadway’s Imperial Theater.

item.title: Runoff Polls in Texas Senate Contest Have Favored Ken Paxton
item.description: Mr. Paxton, a Texas Senate candidate, has led Senator John Cornyn in all but one independent poll in the Republican primary runoff.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton, the Texas state attorney general, received a bump in two polls after being endorsed by President Trump.

item.title: Judge Lifts West Point’s Restrictions on Civilian Professors’ Speech
item.description: A federal judge also said that the government had offered no real justification for limiting the ability of a professor who sued to express opinions in a Military Academy classroom.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: REUTERS/Nathan Howard
media:content.media:description: The Military Academy at West Point cannot limit certain speech of its civilian faculty, a federal judge ruled.
